Sector: # CSV Template: Sector Exposure Scores (S matrix)
# Purpose: Define relative exposure of each sector to climate/economic drivers
# - Sector: Sector name (must match portfolio taxonomy
# - CarbonPrice: Relative exposure to carbon pricing (0-1 scale)
# - CoalPrice: Relative exposure to coal price changes (0-1 scale)
# - OilPrice: Relative exposure to oil price changes (0-1 scale)
# - GasPrice: Relative exposure to natural gas price changes (0-1 scale)
# - GDP: Relative exposure to GDP/macro shocks (0-1 scale)
# - HeatIndex: Relative exposure to heat stress (0-1 scale)
# - FloodRisk: Relative exposure to flood hazards (0-1 scale)
# - DroughtRisk: Relative exposure to drought stress (0-1 scale)
# - Reverse exposure: Use low scores (0.1-0.3) for sectors that BENEFIT from driver increases
# - Energy prices: Based on energy input costs
# - GDP: Based on business cycle sensitivity (discretionary vs essential)
# - Agriculture: Maximum exposure to all physical drivers (water-dependent)
# - Real Estate: Maximum flood exposure (fixed assets in floodplains)
# - Abatement costs: McKinsey sector abatement curves
# - Sensitivity analysis: Vary scores ±30% and check portfolio impact
# - Historical validation: Check if scores align with past energy/climate shocks
# - Sector scores should be reviewed annually or when major regulatory changes occur
# - Updates require documentation of rationale and impact on portfolio EL
# - Model Risk Management approval required for material changes (>0.2 change in any score)
